# DocView

> DocView (alias: mdv) is a Node.js CLI tool that serves a local directory as a rich document viewer in the browser. Run `npx github:simota/docview <directory>` to instantly open a feature-rich viewer with live reload.

## What is DocView?

DocView is an open-source, MIT-licensed Node.js CLI document viewer for developers. It transforms any local directory into a browser-based document viewer with zero configuration.

**One-line definition:** DocView is a local-first, zero-install document viewer CLI for developers that renders Markdown, YAML, JSON, CSV, TSV, JSONL, access logs, and diagrams in the browser with live reload.

## Quick Start

```bash
# No install required
npx github:simota/docview .

# Or install globally
npm install -g github:simota/docview
mdv ./docs
```

## Supported Formats

- **Markdown** (.md) — GFM, footnotes, front-matter, GitHub Alerts, containers
- **Math** — KaTeX ($...$, $$...$$)
- **Diagrams** — Mermaid (flowcharts, sequence, class diagrams, etc.)
- **YAML** (.yaml, .yml) — interactive tree viewer
- **JSON** (.json) — foldable tree viewer
- **CSV / TSV** (.csv, .tsv) — sortable table with Table/Source toggle
- **JSONL / NDJSON** (.jsonl, .ndjson) — streaming JSON table viewer
- **Access Logs** (.log) — Apache/nginx Combined/Common format with status color coding
- **Config** (.toml, .ini, .conf, .env, .cfg) — syntax-highlighted viewer
- **Images** (.png, .jpg, .gif, .webp, .svg)

## Key Features

- **Live Reload** — SSE-based file watching via Chokidar; browser auto-reloads on save
- **Dark Mode** — system preference detection + localStorage persistence
- **File Tree** — hierarchical sidebar navigation
- **Tab Management** — open multiple files simultaneously in tabs
- **Full-Text Search** — grep-based search across entire served directory
- **Vim-style Find Bar** — regex-capable in-page find (`/pattern`)
- **Split View** — view two files side by side
- **Backlinks** — shows which files link to the current file
- **Image Export** — save rendered pages as PNG
- **JSON/YAML Tree Viewer** — collapsible nested structure display
- **Log Viewer** — Apache/nginx access log table with method badges and status color coding
- **Large File Support** — chunked loading with server-side pagination for files over 5 MB
- **In-File Search** — server-side grep with match highlighting for large chunked files
- **Table of Contents** — auto-generated from headings

## Technical Specifications

- **Runtime:** Node.js 18+
- **Architecture:** Stateless `node:http` server + Vanilla TypeScript SPA (no frontend framework)
- **Security:** Path traversal prevention (symlink check), CSP headers, CORS restricted to localhost, DOMPurify HTML sanitization
- **License:** MIT
- **Package:** Available on npm as `docview`

## API Endpoints (Server)

- `GET /api/tree` — recursive file tree JSON
- `GET /api/file?path=` — file content (with security checks); supports `offset` and `limit` params for line-range reads
- `GET /api/file/meta?path=` — file metadata (size, estimated line count) without reading the full file
- `GET /api/file/search?path=&q=` — in-file grep search with pagination for chunked mode
- `GET /api/search?q=` — full-text grep across served directory
- `GET /api/watch` — SSE stream for live reload
- `GET /api/custom-css` — loads `.docview.css` from served directory

## Comparison

| Feature | DocView | VS Code Preview | grip |
|---------|---------|-----------------|------|
| Zero install (npx) | ✓ | No | No |
| Live reload | ✓ SSE | Manual | No |
| Mermaid / KaTeX | ✓ | Extension needed | No |
| YAML/JSON/CSV/TSV/JSONL/Log support | ✓ | No | No |
| Large file chunked loading | ✓ | No | No |
| Full-text search | ✓ | Partial | No |
| Works fully offline | ✓ | ✓ | No (GitHub API) |
